content: General-purpose AI agents can be reoriented around a recursively updated 2025 ICM curiosity mechanism that treats the agent's own expanding long-term memory as an unexplored environment for intrinsic reward, rather than only driving exploration toward external environments. This creates 'curiosity for self-improvement' where the AI agent is intrinsically rewarded for discovering underutilized memory patterns that improve its own downstream task performance, rather than relying solely on extrinsic human-provided task rewards.
